title: veronica pseudolongifolia subsp. borealis
intro: Veronica pseudolongifolia subsp. borealis, also known as boreal speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, characterized by its small blue flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of boreal speedwell include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for respiratory issues. Boreal speedwell can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote respiratory health and ease symptoms of colds and flu.
